steps.title: Find the base angles
steps.content:
Since $AC=CD$, triangle $ACD$ is isosceles. Its vertex angle is $108^\circ$, so each base angle is
$$\frac{180^\circ-108^\circ}{2}=36^\circ$$

steps.title: Use triangle angles
steps.content:
In triangle $BDE$, one angle is $29^\circ$ and another is $36^\circ$. The third angle is
$$180^\circ-29^\circ-36^\circ=115^\circ$$

steps.title: Use the straight angle
steps.content:
The angle marked $x$ forms a straight angle with the $115^\circ$ angle.
$$x=180^\circ-115^\circ=65^\circ$$
The answer is 65.
